title: Fat Freezing in Malta: How Does It Work and What Should You Expect?
excerpt: Carla, 36, had watched a video of fat freezing before her first session and honestly could not understand how sitting still in a clinic could remove fat cells. She arrived sceptical and curious in equal measure.
body_text: Last reviewed by the Carisma Medical Team — 2026 Carla, 36, had watched a video of fat freezing before her first session and honestly could not understand how sitting still in a clinic could remove fat cells. She arrived sceptical and curious in equal measure. If you have ever asked yourself fat freezing Malta how does it work — you are exactly where Carla was. She'd tried the gym phase, the soup diet, the meal replacement shakes — and she was carrying a pocket of stubborn fat just below her navel that seemed completely uninterested in any of it. Someone had mentioned fat freezing Malta to her at a hen do. She'd googled it on the way home, and now here she was, slightly baffled, in a consultation chair. Forty-five minutes into her first session, her therapist began explaining what had just happened inside her tissue — and Carla found herself genuinely fascinated by the science. The fat hadn't budged because it wasn't a diet problem. It was a biology problem. And it turns out, cold is one of the most precise tools in body contouring science. Key Takeaways - Fat freezing (cryolipolysis) uses controlled cooling to destroy stubborn fat cells permanently - Treated fat cells clear through the lymphatic system over 8–12 weeks - A single session reduces the fat layer by around 20–25% in the targeted area - It is a non-invasive body contouring treatment — not a weight loss solution - Results may vary; a free consultation helps confirm whether you are a good candidate What Is Fat Freezing (Cryolipolysis)? Fat freezing — known clinically as cryolipolysis — is a non-surgical, non-invasive body contouring treatment that uses precisely controlled cooling to target and reduce stubborn pockets of subcutaneous fat. It is not a weight loss treatment in the traditional sense; it is a targeted fat reduction treatment designed for localised areas that resist diet and exercise. The treatment was first described in peer-reviewed research by Manstein et al. (2008), and has since been studied extensively across thousands of patients in clinical settings worldwide. Within medically supervised programmes, cryolipolysis is used as a targeted contouring adjunct — most effective when combined with a broader approach to health and body composition. The science: why fat cells freeze before skin does Here is what makes cryolipolysis remarkable: fat cells (adipocytes) are biologically more vulnerable to cold than the skin and surrounding tissue above them. When a controlled cooling panel is applied to the skin surface, the temperature in the subcutaneous fat layer drops to approximately 4°C. At that temperature, adipocytes begin a controlled inflammatory process that triggers programmed cell death — a process called apoptosis. The overlying skin, connective tissue, and nerves are not damaged because they are not susceptible to cold at the same threshold. The selectivity is the science. How the body eliminates frozen fat cells Once the apoptosis cascade has been triggered, the process unfolds over weeks, not days. The body's immune cells — macrophages — identify and engulf the dead fat cells. The freed lipids are transported through the lymphatic system and metabolised normally. This gradual clearance process is why you do not see immediate dramatic changes on the day of treatment. The reduction in fat layer thickness typically becomes measurable at four to eight weeks, with maximum results visible at approximately three months post-treatment. What Happens During a Fat Freezing Session in Malta Understanding the process from the moment you arrive removes a significant amount of anxiety. At our clinic in Malta, we walk every client through exactly what will happen before the applicator is even placed. The applicator, the cooling, and the sensation A gel pad is placed on the treatment area to protect the skin. A hand-piece applicator is then positioned over the targeted area. The applicator uses gentle vacuum suction to draw the tissue between two cooling panels, holding it in place while the controlled cooling begins. In the first few minutes, most people notice an intense cold sensation and a feeling of suction. Some describe a mild aching or tugging. Within approximately five to ten minutes, the area typically becomes numb — and from that point, most clients read, scroll their phones, or simply rest. How long a session lasts A standard cryolipolysis cycle runs for 35 to 60 minutes per treatment area. Multiple areas can be treated in the same appointment where the clinical assessment supports it. The total time in clinic varies depending on how many areas are being addressed, but a single-area session typically fits comfortably into a lunchtime appointment. What the area looks and feels like immediately after Immediately after the applicator is removed, the treated area will appear red and feel firm — occasionally described as a "frozen stick of butter." Our therapist performs a brief massage of the area immediately after treatment, which research suggests may improve the clearance of apoptotic cells. The redness fades within an hour or so. Temporary numbness, tingling, or a mild aching sensation can persist for several days — this is normal, and it reflects the biological process that has been set in motion. Which Areas Can Be Treated? Fat freezing is designed for areas of stubborn subcutaneous fat — the kind that sits beneath the skin and above the muscle. It is not suitable for visceral fat (the fat around the internal organs), and it is not a whole-body treatment. Within medically supervised programmes, our team assesses each area individually to ensure cryolipolysis is the appropriate tool for targeted fat reduction and inch loss in that clinical setting. Abdomen and flanks The lower abdomen and the flanks (commonly called the "love handles" or "muffin top") are among the most commonly treated areas. These areas frequently retain fat even after significant overall weight loss, and they respond well to cryolipolysis in studies. Results may vary for each individual, particularly depending on the composition and volume of fat in the area. Inner and outer thighs Both the inner thigh and outer thigh (the "saddlebags") can be treated with appropriately shaped applicators. The inner thigh is a particularly common concern for women in Malta who find that this area changes shape with age regardless of overall fitness level. Our team will assess the tissue thickness and distribution before recommending treatment here. Upper arms and under-chin The upper arm (the area of soft tissue that can feel loose or full) and the submental area (under the chin) are also treatable with cryolipolysis. These are typically smaller-applicator treatments. The under-chin area, in particular, has been well-studied for cryolipolysis efficacy in peer-reviewed literature. How Many Sessions Do You Need? This is one of the most common questions we hear at our Malta clinic — and the honest answer is that it depends on the area, the volume of tissue present, and your personal goals. What a single session achieves Multiple peer-reviewed studies, including the systematic review by Ingargiola et al. (2015, Aesthetic Surgery Journal), demonstrate an average of approximately 20 to 25% reduction in the subcutaneous fat layer in treated areas per session as measured by ultrasound or MRI. A single session produces a meaningful, measurable change — but it is a change in fat layer thickness in one area, not a dramatic body transformation. That distinction matters, and we will always be clear about it. When multiple sessions are recommended For areas where the fat layer is thicker, or where a client wants more pronounced contouring, a second session in the same area — typically recommended no sooner than eight to twelve weeks after the first, to allow full results to emerge — is often appropriate. Our medical team will make this recommendation based on the initial assessment and the results visible at your follow-up appointment. There is no universal answer, which is exactly why the consultation matters. You can also explore our full range of non-invasive slimming packages to understand where fat freezing fits within a wider body contouring plan. When Will You See Results? The 8-12 week timeline explained Fat freezing does not deliver overnight results. The apoptotic process — the gradual breakdown and clearance of treated fat cells — takes time. Most clients begin to notice a subtle change at around four to eight weeks. The full, measurable result typically emerges at the twelve-week mark, because that is how long it takes for the lymphatic system to fully clear the processed cellular material. Results may vary for each individual based on metabolism, lymphatic function, and baseline fat volume. Why patience is essential with this treatment The timeline can feel slow, especially in an era when we are accustomed to instant feedback. But the gradual nature of the result is also part of what makes it look natural — the contour changes smoothly rather than abruptly. At our clinic, we schedule a follow-up appointment at the eight-to-twelve-week mark so that you can see the actual change, often with comparative photographs and measurements, rather than trying to assess progress by feel alone. The non-scale wins during this period are worth noticing too. Women in our medically supervised programme often find that their waistband sits differently, that their clothes feel more comfortable across the treatment area, that they feel less self-conscious in fitted clothing — weeks before the photographs show the full picture. Is Fat Freezing Safe? Side Effects to Know Within a medically supervised environment, cryolipolysis has a well-established safety profile in a clinical setting. It is important to understand both the common, temporary reactions and the rare risk that every informed client deserves to hear about. Common temporary reactions The most frequently reported side effects are localised to the treatment area and resolve without intervention within days to a few weeks: Redness and bruising — caused by the suction and cooling; typically resolves within a few hours to a week • Temporary numbness — the treated area may feel numb, tingly, or slightly sensitive for up to four weeks • Swelling — mild localised swelling is normal and part of the inflammatory response • Aching or a dull soreness — some clients describe a sensation similar to post-exercise muscle soreness in the treated area for one to two weeks None of these are cause for concern. They reflect the biological process that has been initiated, and they resolve as the body processes the treated tissue. Rare risk: paradoxical adipose hyperplasia — what it is and how rare it is There is one rare adverse event that every client considering fat freezing deserves to know about honestly: paradoxical adipose hyperplasia (PAH). In PAH, the treated area — instead of reducing — grows larger and firmer in a distinct shape. The exact mechanism is not fully understood, but it is thought to involve an atypical tissue response to the cold stimulus. PAH is rare. Published literature places the estimated incidence at approximately 0.0051% (less than one in 20,000 treatment cycles in some studies, though other estimates suggest up to 0.47% depending on the population and device used). It occurs more often in men than in women and appears more frequently in the abdominal area. If it does occur, it is not dangerous — but it is not self-resolving and typically requires surgical intervention. This is why treatment in a clinically supervised setting, with full assessment and informed consent, is essential. At our clinic, we discuss this risk openly before any treatment proceeds. Is Fat Freezing Right for You? Fat freezing works best for people who are at or near their healthy body weight but carry stubborn localised pockets of fat that have resisted other efforts. It is not a first-line treatment for significant weight loss, and it is not a substitute for the lifestyle and metabolic changes that a medically supervised weight loss programme addresses. The ideal candidate is someone like Sarah — whose wedding dress is hanging in the wardrobe, who has been consistent with exercise and eating well, but who has one area that simply will not respond. Or someone like Carla: not far from where she wants to be, but carrying a persistent area of lower abdominal fat that has remained through diet phases and gym phases alike. Results may vary from person to person depending on starting point and individual physiology. Fat freezing is not appropriate for everyone. Clients with cryoglobulinaemia, cold agglutinin disease, paroxysmal cold haemoglobinuria, or Raynaud's phenomenon should not undergo this treatment. Pregnancy and certain skin conditions in the treatment area are also contraindications. Our medically supervised assessment covers all of these before any session is booked. If you are unsure whether fat freezing is the right next step for your goals, the most helpful thing you can do is book a free consultation at Carisma Slimming Malta, where our team will assess your specific situation honestly — without pushing you toward a treatment you do not need. To understand the full picture of what fat freezing involves — including pricing, areas treated, and how it compares to other options — explore our dedicated fat freezing treatment page. FAQs About Fat Freezing in Malta Does fat freezing hurt in Malta? Most clients do not find fat freezing painful, though the first few minutes of each session involve intense cold and suction that can feel uncomfortable. The common description is: cold, then a tight pulling sensation, then numbness. Once the area is numb — which typically happens within five to ten minutes — most people relax, read, or scroll their phones. A brief massage immediately after the session is the most uncomfortable moment for some clients, lasting two to three minutes. Overall, the experience is well-tolerated and does not require anaesthesia or recovery time. How long do fat freezing results last? Cryolipolysis destroys fat cells — and fat cells, once eliminated through apoptosis and lymphatic clearance, do not regenerate in adults. The treated area's fat cell count is permanently reduced. However, the remaining fat cells in the body — including in the treated area — can still expand with weight gain. The longevity of your results is closely tied to maintaining a stable weight. In clients who maintain their weight after treatment, the contouring results are considered long-lasting. Within our medically supervised programme, we support you with the tools to protect your investment. Can fat freezing remove a lot of fat? Fat freezing is a body contouring treatment, not a weight loss solution. It reduces the subcutaneous fat layer in a targeted area by approximately 20 to 25% per session on average, as shown in peer-reviewed studies including Ingargiola et al. (2015, Aesthetic Surgery Journal). This translates to noticeable contouring in the treated area — a softer edge to the flank, less fullness in the lower abdomen — rather than a change in overall body weight. If your goal involves meaningful weight reduction, a medically supervised weight loss programme may be a more appropriate first step, and our team will tell you that honestly. Is there any downtime after fat freezing in Malta? There is no formal downtime required after fat freezing. Most clients return to normal daily activities immediately after their session. You may notice temporary redness, swelling, or numbness in the treated area for a few days to a couple of weeks, but these do not typically interfere with work, driving, or normal life. Some clients prefer to schedule sessions for late afternoon so that any temporary redness has settled by the following morning. There is no need to take time off work, and you can resume most exercise within 24 to 48 hours. What is paradoxical adipose hyperplasia and should I be worried? Paradoxical adipose hyperplasia (PAH) is a rare adverse event where the treated fat area grows larger and firmer rather than reducing. It affects an estimated fraction of a percent of treatments — and appears more frequently in men than in women. It is not dangerous but does require separate medical attention if it occurs. We explain this risk at every consultation because we believe every client deserves honest information before agreeing to any treatment. The fact that it can happen — and that we tell you about it openly — is part of what a medically supervised clinical environment looks like, as opposed to an unsupervised beauty setting. Can I exercise after a fat freezing session? Most clients can return to gentle exercise within 24 hours of a fat freezing session. The treated area may feel tender or numb for a few days, so high-impact activity directly targeting that area is worth avoiding until the sensitivity settles. There is no evidence that exercise negatively affects the treatment outcome — and maintaining regular activity and a stable weight during the 8-to-12-week result period is actively beneficial. If you are unsure what is appropriate for your specific session and treatment area, our team will give you personalised guidance before you leave the clinic. Your Next Step You deserve information that is honest — and support that is actually built around you, not around a one-size-fits-all sales script. Fat freezing in Malta is a real, evidence-based option for localised fat reduction in targeted areas. It is not magic, and it works best within a broader, thoughtful approach to your body and health. Picture yourself in twelve weeks: your clothes fitting differently in that one area that has frustrated you for years. Feeling less self-conscious in fitted outfits. Walking into the next event without adjusting your clothing or angles. That is achievable — with the right assessment, the right expectations, and the right clinical team beside you. If you would like to explore whether cryolipolysis is appropriate for your goals, book a free consultation with our team at Carisma Slimming Malta. No pressure. No hard sell. Just an honest conversation about what is possible for you specifically. You can also explore our full range of non-surgical slimming and body contouring treatments to understand how fat freezing fits within a complete approach to your goals.
